author: potuz
content: For a NO it doesn't need to be. It can just default to all builders being treated the same way with a default value

author: serenita_io
content:
I agree we should standardize new CLI flags, that has historically been a pain point with clients calling things slightly differently.

**Beacon API**

I would get rid of `min_bid` since the more recent `builder_boost_factor` allows for almost exactly the same thing. Why support two extremely similar things? It's just confusing and overcomplicated. Seems like a good time to say goodbye to `min_bid` which is –in my opinion– the worse of the two options.

**VC configuration**

> Standardized file format

I believe new per-validator-key config options should be handled by extending the Keymanager API. That is exactly what this API is for, right? *(I strongly dislike passing and updating yaml files, much easier to call the standardized API).*

In general, I hope we can keep things reasonably simple, and that we don't overcomplicate all of our codebases to cater to the specific usecases of one or two node operators in the world.

author: dgusakov
content: Would trusted bids be available only via a direct builder connection? Or will they also be propagated via P2P?

author: serenita_io
content:
Having thought about it some more, I want to float the question whether we even really need this amount of customization.

1) Setting these builder-specific options on a per-validator level seems excessive. Do we expect someone to run hundreds of keys on a single validator client, with tens of different per-key builder configurations? Just because we could in theory support this, doesn't mean we should if it's going to go unused.

2) Setting different per-builder preferences. Is this something someone asked for, or something we know will be used? And if we forego the option to set this at a per-validator level, couldn't this simply be set on the beacon node side, without requiring API changes?

I'm questioning whether the minor configurability benefits outweigh the development and maintenance burden here. In general I'd lean towards keeping things as simple as possible.

author: jameshe5018
content: currently builder configs are at a per key level in prysm, the question is node operators if this is important vs something general to the bn. I'm not familiar with how much customization operators allow their validators
